canonical_definition: A discrepancy between the formal objective used for training a model and the intended goal, causing the model to learn behaviors that are not truly aligned with user preferences.
papers: Geometric-Averaged Preference Optimization for Soft Preference Labels
Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overoptimization in Direct Alignment Algorithms

definitions.text: The extent to which a learned reward function contains spurious correlations or preferences that do not match the intended objective.
